evals.name: skewed-classes-iaa-trap
evals.prompt: Two annotators labeled 1000 tweets for hate-speech (yes/no). Cohen κ = 0.32. Is annotation quality bad?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NOT necessarily — flag the skewed-prevalence problem (most tweets are 'no'); (2) recommend computing PABAK or F1-complement for the rare-class agreement; (3) Cohen κ underestimates real agreement on skewed-prevalence tasks; (4) report per-class IAA + raw agreement %; (5) recommend Krippendorff α as alternative.

evals.name: calibration-skipping-cost
evals.prompt: We have 50 hours of annotator time + a 10K-item dataset to label. Should we just go straight to bulk to maximize coverage?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — calibration round is non-negotiable; (2) explain cost asymmetry (skipping calibration → 10-20% bulk re-annotation = 1000-2000 items wasted); (3) recommend 50 items pilot + 100 items calibration before bulk; (4) cite ~5-7 hours total for calibration vs 50+ hours for re-annotation; (5) note 10% double-annotated for IAA monitoring during bulk.

evals.name: ner-span-iaa-correct-metric
evals.prompt: I'm building a low-resource Yoruba NER dataset with 3 annotators. What IAA metric should I use?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recommend γ (gamma) — span/unitized task (NER); (2) explain κ doesn't model span boundaries; (3) note Krippendorff α as alternative for label agreement portion; (4) target ≥ 0.8; (5) report bootstrap CI; (6) recommend INCEpTION or Prodigy for tooling; (7) cross-skill: route to magic-linguistic-ethics for community-data CARE/FPIC consideration.
